atomizationResults.rawInstruction: CH 12
1 DC in each stitch all the way around (24)
2HTCtog, 1HTC, 1HTC, CH2, skip the next 2 stitches, 1HTC, 1HTC, 2HTCtog, CH2, turn (8)
work 12 treble crochet, join. (12)
Weave in the ends.
With CC1, ch 3, 2 dc in loop, ch 2, * 3 dc in loop, ch 2; rep from * twice more, join with sl st in top of beginning ch 3. Break CC1.

validationIssues.message: actionTag must be camelCase letters or digits (got sl st).
validationIssues.sourceText: join with sl st in top of beginning ch 3

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Weave in ends.

Size S/M/L ONLY: Work across two sides of square 21, joining to triangle 14 and square 15, and one side of square 22, joining to square 15. Size XL/2X/3X ONLY: Work across two sides of square 33, joining to triangle 22 and square 23, then across two sides of square 34, joining to squares 23 and 24, and one side of square 35, joining to square 24. Both sizes: Work across the next side of square 22 (35) without joining, then work across one side of square 23 (36), joining to the side you just worked on square 22 (35). Continue across the next side of square 23 (36), and two sides of squares 24 and 25 (37–40), joining to the squares below, then work one side of square 26 (41) joining to square 18 (29). Work across the next side of square 26 (41) without joining, then work across one side of square 27 (42), joining to the side you just worked on square 26 (41). Work second side of square 27 (42) and last 1 (2) squares of this tier as established, ending with square 28 (44). Work across tops of all squares in this tier beginning with square 28 (44) and ending with square 21 (33). Fasten off.
Repeat the last 2 rows until hem measures 2½" / 6.5 cm. Bind off in rib.
Sew buttons to correspond with buttonholes. Weave in all ends and block as desired.
Create a magic ring with 6 sc in the ring. Close the ring.
(7 sc, inc) x 3 (27 sc)
Using the tapestry needle, sew in the magic ring's 6" tail.

validationIssues.sourceText: Using the tapestry needle, sew in the magic ring's 6" tail.

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Repeat Rnd 1 until you have 15 sc left.
Slightly overstuff with Poly-fil (or other fiber fill). Do not close hole as it is difficult to reopen. Tuck tail into Knocker so that filling can be adjusted as needed.

validationIssues.sourceText: Slightly overstuff with Poly-fil (or other fiber fill). Do not close hole as it is difficult to reopen. Tuck tail into Knocker so that filling can be adjusted as needed.

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Ch 17
Sc in 2nd ch from hook and in next 2 chs. Hdc in next ch, dc in next 8 chains. Hdc in next ch. Sc in last 3 chains. Turn (16)
Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts.
Finish off and sew the 2 ends together.
Weave in all ends.
Into a magic ring: ch 1, make 6 sc, join to the first sc with a sl st. {6 sc}
(FPhdc around the FPhdc, ch 4, make a Popcorn in the next ch-2 sp, ch 4) 6 times. Join to the first FPhdc with a sl st. {6 FPhdc, 6 Popcorns, and 12 ch-4 sps}

Ch 1 (doesn't count as a st). (FPdc around the next st from Round 5, skip the first st of the next Petal from Round 6 and hdc in the next 2 sts. [Hdc, ch 2, hdc] in the next ch-2 sp. Hdc in the next 2 sts, skip the last st of the Petal) 12 times. Mark the last ch-2 sp with a SM. Join to the first FPdc with a sl st. Fasten off and work away your ends. {72 hdc, 12 FPdc, and 12 ch-2 sps}

Ch 1 (doesn't count as a st). *Make 7 sc in the next ch-6 sp, [2 hdc, 2 dc, 2 edc] in the next ch-6 sp. [2 tr, ch 2, 2 tr] in the next st. [2 edc, 2 dc, 2 hdc] in the next ch-6 sp.* Rep from * to * 3 more times. Join to the first sc with a sl st. Fasten off and work away your ends.

validationIssues.sourceText: Fasten off and work away your ends

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Sl st in the next st and the next ch-2 sp. Ch 3 (counts as dc). [Dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in the same ch-2 sp. This is your first corner made. *Dc in the next 30 sts. [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in the next ch-2 sp.* Rep from * to * 3 more times, omitting the last corner on the last repeat. Join to the top of the beg ch-3 with a sl st. Fasten off and work away your ends. {34 dc, not including ch-2 corner sps}
CH 4
DEC, 1 SC, DEC (3)
Place a stitch marker in the space between Row 14 and Row 15. (This is where you will begin working the second arm)
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Place stitch marker for second arm

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Attach yarn between Rows 11–12. CH 1, SC in same sp, HDC in next sp, 3 DC in next sp, CH 2, SS in same sp. FO

Place both panels together with wrong sides facing each other. Attach yarn at any point along the edge. Work slip stitches evenly around, inserting your hook through both layers. Continue around the entire shape to join. Optional: Lightly stuff the bear before fully closing, or leave flat for a keychain-style finish. Fasten off and weave in all ends securely.
in a MR, sc 6. (6)
sc around. (12)
Finish off and sew closed.
With off white, ch 114. 1 sc in the 2nd ch from the hook and in each ch across. (113 sc) Ch 1, turn.
1 sc in each st across, changing to light blue in the last sc. Ch 1, turn.
1 sc in each sc across. Ch 1, turn.
Weave in ends, then move on to the border.
Ch 1, 1 sc in each st around, place 3 sc in each corner. Join with a sl st to the first sc. Finish off. Weave in ends.
Start 10 sc in a magic ring
2 dec, 10 sc = 12 sc
dec, 9 sc = 10 sc
Next, insert the wire into the body, pre-bend the edges of the wire
We glue the eyes and embroider the eyebrows.

with green, make a slip knot and ch 14; sl st in the first ch you made, to join the piece in a circle; [ch 3, sl st in the next ch of the circle] 14 times (work your last sl st into the st that joined the circle) (14 chain-loops made)
Fasten off and weave in the ends (or keep them for sewing, if the wreath will become an applique).
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Fasten off and weave in ends

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Take a 30-40cm/12-16" length of red yarn, thread it into a yarn needle and weave it through the chain loops around the wreath, starting and ending at the join. Tie the ends in a bow, then cut them to the length you like.
Optionally, if your yarn holds knots poorly or tends to fray at the ends, dot some clear nail polish or white glue at the knot and at the ends, to secure them.
With grey yarn: Magic loop, ch1, 7sc, slst to the first sc.

Ch2 (count as 1hdc), 3hdc, (hdc inc)x3; Change to white yarn: 3hdc, 2sc, (sc inc, 4sc) repeat 2 times, sc inc, 2sc, 3hdc; Change back to grey yarn: (hdc inc)x3. Slst to the top of the first ch2, and fasten off.
With grey stitches on top, join white yarn to the 4th grey stitch from the left. Ch2, (1dc, ch-1 pic, 1dc) in the next st, ch2, slst to the next st. Fasten off and weave in the ends.
Ch1+1sc, 1sc in each st around, slst to the first sc. Fasten off, leave a long tail for attaching.
Make two French knots for the eyes. Then, use pink yarn to stitch the blushes just below the eyes for a finished look.
CH 12. Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain, work as follows.
Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth.
Now we will work our button hole.
work 12 treble crochet, join.
Weave in the ends.
Chain 11, turn and crochet to the second chain from hook.
Inc, 8sc, 4inc, 8sc, 3inc. (32)
To connect 2 shells, use 10slst. FLO from the top shell, BLO from bottom shell.
Sew all parts, nose, eyes and cheeks to finish.
Create a magic ring with 6 sc in the ring. Close the ring.
(7 sc, inc) x 3 (27 sc)

Select the cup size and continue. A cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 three times until 4.5" across or 13 rounds (approx. 63 sc). B cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 four times until 5" across or 15 rounds (approx.72 sc). C cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 five times until 5.5" across or 17 rounds (approx. 81 sc). D cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 six times until 6" across or 19 rounds (approx 90 sc). DD cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 seven times until 6.5" across or 21 rounds (approx. 99 sc).
(inv dec (PM), inv dec, then sc until 1 sc before the next marker) x 3.
If stuffing: slightly overstuff with Poly-fil, do not close hole, tuck tail into Knocker. If mailing unstuffed: leave hole open and yarn tail outside.
Ch 17
Sc in 2nd ch from hook and in next 2 chs. Hdc in next ch, dc in next 8 chains. Hdc in next ch. Sc in last 3 chains. Turn (16)
Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts.
Finish off and sew the 2 ends together.
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Finish off and sew the 2 ends together

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Weave in all ends.
Into a magic ring: ch 1, make 6 sc, join to the first sc with a sl st. {6 sc}
(FPhdc around the FPhdc, ch 4, make a Popcorn in the next ch-2 sp, ch 4) 6 times. Join to the first FPhdc with a sl st. {6 FPhdc, 6 Popcorns, and 12 ch-4 sps}

Ch 1 (doesn't count as a st). (FPdc around the next st from Round 5, skip the first st of the next Petal from Round 6 and hdc in the next 2 sts. [Hdc, ch 2, hdc] in the next ch-2 sp. Hdc in the next 2 sts, skip the last st of the Petal) 12 times. Mark the last ch-2 sp with a SM. Join to the first FPdc with a sl st. Fasten off and work away your ends. {72 hdc, 12 FPdc, and 12 ch-2 sps}

Ch 1 (doesn't count as a st). *Make 7 sc in the next ch-6 sp, [2 hdc, 2 dc, 2 edc] in the next ch-6 sp. [2 tr, ch 2, 2 tr] in the next st. [2 edc, 2 dc, 2 hdc] in the next ch-6 sp.* Rep from * to * 3 more times. Join to the first sc with a sl st. Fasten off and work away your ends. Stitch Count per Side: 23 sts {7 sc, 4 hdc, 4 dc, 4 edc, and 4 tr, not including ch-2 corner sps}

Sl st in the next st and the next ch-2 sp. Ch 3 (counts as dc). [Dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in the same ch-2 sp. This is your first corner made. *Dc in the next 30 sts. [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in the next ch-2 sp.* Rep from * to * 3 more times, omitting the last corner on the last repeat. Join to the top of the beg ch-3 with a sl st. Fasten off and work away your ends. {34 dc, not including ch-2 corner sps}
Ch 4.
DEC, 1 SC, DEC (3)
DEC, 7 SC, DEC (9)
Attach yarn between Rows 11-12. CH 1, SC in same sp, HDC in next sp, 3 DC in next sp, CH 2, SS in same sp. FO.
Fasten off and weave in all ends securely.
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Fasten off and weave in ends

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: in a MR, sc 6. (6)
sc around. (12)
Finish off and sew closed.
1 sc in the 2nd ch from the hook and in each ch across. (113 sc) Ch 1, turn.
1 sc in each st across, changing to light blue in the last sc. Ch 1, turn.
1 sc in each sc across,changing to teal in the last sc. Ch 1, turn.
Ch 1, turn, and do one more row of sc.
Ch 1, 1 sc in each st around, place 3 sc in each corner. Join with a sl st to the first sc. Finish off. Weave in ends.
Start 10 sc in a magic ring
dec, 8 sc = 9 sc
12 sc
dec, 13 sc = 14 sc
Gluing the eyes: I smeared the tips of the eyes with glue and inserted them in 10 rows from the beginning of the head, the distance between the eyes is 10 loops. We embroider eyebrows over the eyes.

with green, make a slip knot and ch 14; sl st in the first ch you made, to join the piece in a circle; [ch 3, sl st in the next ch of the circle] 14 times (work your last sl st into the st that joined the circle) (14 chain-loops made)
Fasten off and weave in the ends (or keep them for sewing, if the wreath will become an applique).
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Fasten off and weave in the ends

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Take a 30-40cm/12-16" length of red yarn, thread it into a yarn needle and weave it through the chain loops around the wreath, starting and ending at the join. Tie the ends in a bow, then cut them to the length you like.
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Weave red yarn through chain loops and tie a bow

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: Optionally, if your yarn holds knots poorly or tends to fray at the ends, dot some clear nail polish or white glue at the knot and at the ends, to secure them.
atomizationResults.roundTitle: Optional: secure knot with nail polish or glue

atomizationResults.rawInstruction: With grey yarn: Magic loop, ch1, 7sc, slst to the first sc.

Ch2 (count as 1hdc), 1hdc in the same st to complete the first hdc inc, (hdc inc)x3; Change to white yarn: 1hdc, 1hdc+1sc, 1sc, sc inc, 9sc, sc inc, 1sc, 1sc+1hdc, 1hdc; Change back to grey yarn: hdc inc. Slst to the top of the first ch2.
With grey stitches on top, join white yarn to the 4th grey stitch from the left. Ch2, (1dc, ch-1 pic, 1dc) in the next st, ch2, slst to the next st. Fasten off and weave in the ends.
Magic loop, ch2(count as 1hdc), 9hdc, slst to the top of the first ch2.
Make two French knots for the eyes. Then, use pink yarn to stitch the blushes just below the eyes for a finished look.
